name: Pediatric Glasgow Coma Scale
description: Assesses impaired consciousness and coma in pediatric patients.
purpose: Diagnosis of impaired consciousness and coma in preverbal children
when_to_use: Infants with head trauma, altered mental status or neurologic abnormalities
Use in initial and serial assessments
when_not_to_use: Children older than 2 years (use the standard Glasgow Coma Scale instead)

items.label: verbal_response
items.description: Verbal Response (age-appropriate for preverbal children ≤2 years)

items.label: motor_response
items.description: Motor Response (age-appropriate for preverbal children ≤2 years)

interpretation.description: Severe head injury. Consider intubation/definitive airway management.

references.citation: James HE. Neurologic evaluation and support in the child with an acute brain insult. Pediatr Ann. 1986 Jan;15(1):16-22.

notes: Use for children 2 years and younger only. For older children, use the standard Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS). Should be reported as a sum and include the scores of each of the individual components (e.g., Total pGCS 12=E3+V4+M5). In intubated patients, consider using the Full Outline of UnResponsiveness (FOUR) score. The score alone is not sufficient to guide diagnosis or management. All patients with a GCS <15 need appropriate monitoring.
